key_departures.impact: Traded to the Jets along with a 2026 seventh-round pick in exchange for a 2026 sixth-round pick. Smith had been acquired mid-2025 via trade with Seattle. Trade clears the path for the Raiders to select QB Fernando Mendoza with the No. 1 overall pick.
Starting guard departed to the Jets in free agency. Was a key piece of the interior line but the Raiders are aggressively rebuilding the IOL around Linderbaum and Jackson Powers-Johnson.
Released by the Raiders. Veteran guard who spent 2025 in Las Vegas. Depth loss at IOL but not a starter-level hole.
Started 13 games at left tackle filling in for injured Kolton Miller in 2025. The Raiders had no plans to bring him back with Miller expected to return healthy. Depth loss only.

Backup QB who appeared in six games (two starts) for the Raiders after being acquired from Cleveland mid-2025. His departure is by design — the Raiders are clearing the QB room ahead of drafting Mendoza No. 1 overall.
Safety signed with the Dolphins on a 1-year deal. Not a priority for the Raiders; missed time due to a broken fibula in training camp. Rotational/depth loss only.
Linebacker who was not a high priority for the Raiders. His departure is inconsequential given the massive LB overhaul via Dean and Walker signings.
The Raiders' all-time No. 2 scorer was let go after the team signed Matt Gay. Carlson was a reliable long-tenured kicker; his departure is offset immediately by the Gay signing.

signings.analysis: Record-setting contract makes Linderbaum the highest-paid interior offensive lineman in NFL history at $27M AAV, surpassing Creed Humphrey's $18M/yr by a staggering margin. The deal is effectively fully guaranteed ($80M of $81M). Linderbaum is a three-time Pro Bowler who has started 71 of a possible 73 games and posted a 96.2% pass block win rate since 2022. His arrival anchors the IOL rebuild around Jackson Powers-Johnson (now locked in at right guard) in preparation for rookie QB Fernando Mendoza. IOL goes from a glaring top-tier need to solved at center. Guard depth still needed but the critical center hole is emphatically closed.

Acquired via trade (Raiders sent 2026 sixth-round pick, received Johnson plus a 2026 seventh-round pick). Johnson is a veteran nickel/slot CB who was a Second-Team All-Pro in 2023 and brings 572 career tackles, six interceptions, eight forced fumbles and eight sacks across 113 career games. However, his 1-year contract at $1.6M is almost certainly a prove-it deal at the back end of his career — this is a bridge/depth-level financial commitment despite his starting pedigree. His age (29, turns 30 in-season) and the lack of guaranteed money beyond this season signal the Raiders will look for a longer-term answer at CB in the draft. Partially addresses slot CB need but not a long-term solution.

Re-signing Stokes to a 3-year/$30M deal with $20M guaranteed locks in a corner who had a strong bounce-back season in 2025 after his injury-plagued stint with Green Bay, starting a career-high 16 games and logging 53 tackles and five passes defensed. At $10M AAV, this sits firmly in the mid-tier starter range for CB. The 3-year commitment and $20M guaranteed signals genuine investment. Prevents a secondary hole from opening and gives the Raiders a credible starting CB2/CB1. However, Stokes has not yet proven he is an elite CB1; the Raiders likely still want to address CB further through the draft. Partially addresses CB need — avoids a crisis scenario but doesn't fully close the position out.

Signed to a 3-year/$48M deal with $31.28M guaranteed. Paye is a former first-round pick (21st overall, 2021) who started 74 of 75 games across five seasons with the Colts and totaled 30.5 career sacks. His 2025 was a down year (4 sacks, his lowest since his rookie season) but he showed a career-high 23 pressures. At $16M AAV, this is a mid-tier EDGE commitment — meaningful investment but not top-of-market. With the Maxx Crosby trade collapsing (Crosby remains on the roster), Paye slots in as a complementary EDGE rusher rather than the lead star. However, ESPN analysis notes Las Vegas will still need another pass rusher on the opposite side of Paye. EDGE is partially addressed — Paye + Crosby (if retained) is a solid pair, but if Crosby is eventually traded, the Raiders will need more at the position.

Re-signed on a 1-year prove-it deal after bouncing back from a season-ending knee injury in 2024. Koonce appeared in all 17 games in 2025 and tallied 4.5 sacks and 13 QB hits. He had a breakout 2023 (8 sacks) before the ACL tear. At $11M on a single year, this is pure bridge/depth with high upside optionality. Has a relationship with new DC Rob Leonard. His one-year structure means the Raiders don't view him as their long-term EDGE answer — he is insurance and depth alongside Paye and (for now) Crosby. Minimal need impact on its own; the Paye signing does more work.

signings.impact_note: Combined assessment with Walker signing; see analysis.
signings.analysis: Three-year deal at $12M AAV with $23M guaranteed per Spotrac. Dean is 25 years old, a known blitzer who played a key role in Philadelphia's Super Bowl LIX championship defense in 2024, posting career highs in tackles (128), TFLs (9), and fumble recoveries (2). Has durability concerns (47 games across 4 seasons) but is elite when healthy. The three-year commitment and majority-guaranteed structure signal genuine starter investment. Combined with the Quay Walker signing below, the Raiders have effectively rebuilt their LB room from scratch — replacing Devin White/Jamal Adams/Elandon Roberts with two credentialed starters. LB was a critical need; it is now fully addressed.

Three-year/$40.5M deal with $28M guaranteed per Spotrac. Walker led the Packers in tackles in all four of his NFL seasons, topped 100 tackles in each campaign, and posted a career-high 128 tackles in 2025 with a low 4.5% missed tackle rate. At 25, he has significant upside, though his coverage deficiencies are a legitimate concern. At $13.5M AAV (seventh-highest paid ILB), this is a full-value starter commitment. Paired with Nakobe Dean, the Raiders have invested over $76M in guaranteed money across three years to remake the LB position wholesale. Together, Dean and Walker fully address what ESPN called the 'biggest need on the roster.' LB is no longer a draft priority.

Three-year/$35M deal with $23M guaranteed per Spotrac. At $11.7M AAV, this sits at the bottom of starter-tier WR money but well above depth. Nailor averaged 15.4 yards per reception over four Vikings seasons, posting career highs in receptions (29) and yards (444) in 2025. He is a speed-first receiver with explosive upside who was buried behind Jefferson and Addison in Minnesota. For a rookie QB in Fernando Mendoza, Nailor's reliability (career 98.8 passer rating when targeted with J.J. McCarthy) is valuable. However, the Raiders' WR room remains thin — Nailor, Tre Tucker, Jack Bech, and Donte Thornton Jr. is not an elite supporting cast. WR need is partially addressed: the signing is meaningful but the Raiders still need at least one more credible pass-catcher, potentially via the draft.

1-year deal replacing Daniel Carlson, who was allowed to walk. Gay has an 84.3% career field goal percentage across seven seasons but was only 73.9% in 2025 across 12 games split between Washington and San Francisco. He has history with new Raiders special teams coordinator Joe DeCamillis (they won Super Bowl LVI together with the Rams). A lateral move at kicker — replaces one veteran starter with another. No meaningful impact on positional needs picture.

notable_roster_context.maxx_crosby_trade_collapse: The Raiders agreed to trade EDGE Maxx Crosby to the Baltimore Ravens for two first-round picks (2026 No. 14 overall and a 2027 first) on March 6. The deal collapsed on March 11 when the Ravens backed out citing medical concerns related to Crosby's knee (torn meniscus repaired in January). Crosby remains on the Las Vegas roster under contract at $30M for 2026. This significantly complicates the Raiders' cap picture — they committed ~$281.5M in free agency contracts partly predicated on Crosby's $30M salary coming off the books. With Crosby retained, the Raiders still have over $37M in remaining cap space per Over the Cap. The team has indicated all free agent deals will be honored and has signaled openness to revisiting a Crosby trade with other suitors. Crosby's status as starter vs. trade candidate remains fluid as of the updated_through date.
notable_roster_context.maxx_crosby_current_status: Remains on Raiders roster; trade to Baltimore officially voided. Team has not ruled out future trade. His presence means the EDGE position is well-stocked (Crosby + Paye + Koonce) even though the long-term plan for him is uncertain.
notable_roster_context.jackson_powers_johnson: Incumbent RG whose starting role was solidified by the Linderbaum signing. Coach Kubiak's previous uncertainty about his role resolved — he is the starting right guard.
notable_roster_context.kolton_miller: Starting LT who missed significant time in 2025 with injury but is under contract and expected to return healthy. Stone Forsythe's departure does not create a new OT hole.
notable_roster_context.draft_context: Raiders hold the No. 1 overall pick, widely expected to be used on Indiana QB Fernando Mendoza. They also hold a 2026 sixth-round pick received from the Jets in the Geno Smith trade. The Ravens' two first-round picks were voided with the Crosby trade collapse.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Tyler Linderbaum (3yr/$81M, $80M guaranteed) is the best center in the NFL and makes this the most locked-in position on the roster. Jackson Powers-Johnson is entrenched at RG. The only remaining IOL need is guard depth — likely a Day 2/3 draft pick or late signing. Do not treat IOL as a draft priority at premium picks.

Nakobe Dean (3yr/$36M, $23M gtd) and Quay Walker (3yr/$40.5M, $28M gtd) represent over $76M in guaranteed money and fully remake the LB room. Both are 25 years old on multi-year deals — this is a long-term solution, not a bridge. LB is no longer a premium draft need. At most, a Day 3 depth addition.

Fernando Mendoza is the near-certain No. 1 overall pick. Geno Smith and Kenny Pickett have been traded/departed. No current need at QB beyond finding a veteran backup after the draft, which will likely happen via late free agency or camp invites.

With the Crosby trade collapse, Las Vegas retains their best EDGE rusher alongside new additions Paye (3yr/$48M) and Koonce (1yr/$11M). The EDGE room is solid for 2026. However, Crosby's long-term future in Las Vegas is uncertain (trade still possible), and Koonce is only on a 1-year deal. A Day 2 developmental EDGE is still advisable to build future depth, but this is no longer a crisis need. Reduce from critical to moderate.

Eric Stokes re-signed (3yr/$30M, $20M gtd) provides CB1/CB2 continuity. Taron Johnson (1yr/$1.6M) adds veteran slot depth but is a short-term bridge. The secondary still lacks a true lockdown CB1 and Johnson's 1-year deal means the slot needs a long-term answer. CB remains a meaningful draft need — a Day 1 or 2 pick at the position is warranted.

Jalen Nailor (3yr/$35M) upgrades the WR room but does not solve it — he is a WR2/speed threat, not a true No. 1. The current room of Nailor, Tre Tucker, Jack Bech, and Thornton Jr. is below average for a team building around a rookie QB. WR remains a meaningful need for both free agency continuation and the draft, particularly in Rounds 1-3.

Kolton Miller returns healthy at LT; Stone Forsythe's departure was expected and doesn't open a new hole. However, RT depth and long-term OT depth behind Miller remain thin. Unchanged — still a Day 2/3 consideration.

No significant IDL additions or departures in free agency. Thomas Booker received an RFA tender. The Raiders' 3-4 base under DC Rob Leonard will need quality IDL talent. Unchanged need — Day 2/3 draft priority.
Lonnie Johnson Jr. departed but was not a key starter. No replacement signed. Safety remains a background need — the Raiders should look at Day 3 or veteran minimum options.

key_additions.headline: Record-setting center deal makes Linderbaum highest-paid interior lineman in NFL history, anchoring the rebuild around rookie QB Fernando Mendoza
Super Bowl champion LB brings elite blitzing ability and championship pedigree to overhaul a Raiders linebacker room that was a major weakness
Four-year tackling machine (100+ tackles each season) reunites with Georgia teammate Dean to give Las Vegas its most credentialed LB duo in years
First-round pedigree EDGE rusher (30.5 career sacks) gives Raiders a legitimate starter alongside Maxx Crosby as they reshape the pass rush
Raiders lock up bounce-back CB who started a career-high 16 games in 2025, maintaining secondary continuity heading into the Mendoza era
Former Second-Team All-Pro nickel CB acquired via trade (6th/7th pick swap) adds veteran savvy to the slot
Explosive speed threat (15.4 ypc career avg) gives rookie QB Fernando Mendoza a reliable vertical weapon and security blanket
Proven 8-sack pass rusher returns on a prove-it deal as he continues recovery from 2024 ACL tear, providing EDGE depth and upside
Veteran kicker reunites with Raiders special teams coordinator Joe DeCamillis, replacing longtime franchise kicker Daniel Carlson

key_losses.headline: QB trade to Jets clears the decks for the Raiders to select Fernando Mendoza No. 1 overall in April's draft
Starting guard's departure creates depth gap at IOL, though Linderbaum signing addresses the more critical center void
All-time No. 2 scorer in Raiders history let go as new regime pivots to Matt Gay at the kicking position
Backup LT who started 13 games in 2025 for injured Miller signs with Carolina; departure expected with Miller healthy
Backup QB departure confirms Raiders are going all-in on Mendoza at No. 1 overall; leaves team without a backup until further action

confidence_notes: All contract figures sourced from Spotrac and NFL.com/ESPN reports. The Linderbaum $80M guaranteed figure ($60M per original reports, $80M per Spotrac breakdown) reflects a fully-guaranteed structure over 3 years per Spotrac's detailed breakdown. The Maxx Crosby trade voiding is confirmed. Cap space estimates are approximate pending final accounting of all contract structures against the $301.2M 2026 cap. Paye guaranteed figure ($31.28M per Spotrac) vs. earlier ESPN report of $32M is a minor rounding discrepancy — using Spotrac as primary source. Gay and Koonce 1-year figures sourced from Spotrac and NFL.com respectively.
